title: Elymus × cayouetteorum
taxonomy: Elymus × cayouetteorum is a hybrid species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is believed to be a cross between Elymus caninus and possibly another species of Elymus. The classification of Elymus × cayouetteorum is not well-documented, and further research is needed to confirm its hybrid origins. However, it is generally classified as a member of the Elymus genus, which includes many species of grasses native to North America and Eurasia. intro: Elymus × cayouetteorum, also known as Cayouette's wheat grass or a hybrid of wheatgrass, is a type of perennial grass in the family Poaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Elymus × cayouetteorum include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of stress and anxiety.
